QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_20

question.text: Which type of equity fund must invest a minimum of 65% in equity across large, mid, and small cap stocks with at least 25% each in large and mid cap?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI's Multi Cap Fund category mandates a minimum 75% in equity (changed from 65% by SEBI circular) with at least 25% each in large cap, mid cap, and small cap stocks. This ensures genuine diversification across market caps. **The Trap:** Flexi Cap sounds similar but has NO mandatory minimum allocation to any specific cap segment. Multi Cap has the 25-25-25 rule making it distinct. **Cross-Exam Context:** The Multi Cap vs Flexi Cap distinction was introduced when SEBI created the Flexi Cap category in 2020. This is a hot topic across NISM Series V-A and II-B exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Multi Cap funds are genuinely diversified across all caps due to the mandatory 25% minimums. Flexi Cap gives the fund manager freedom to tilt towards any cap segment based on market conditions. Choose based on whether the client wants structured diversification or manager discretion.
